questions.question: Q1. What is the primary objective of traffic signal optimization?

answers.a: To improve traffic flow and reduce congestion

explanations.a: Correct. Traffic signal optimization aims to improve traffic movement and reduce congestion.
explanations.b: The goal is to minimize waiting time.
explanations.c: Road width is unrelated to signal optimization.
explanations.d: Efficient traffic flow generally reduces fuel consumption.

questions.question: Q2. Which input parameter represents the number of vehicles arriving at an intersection per minute?

explanations.a: Queue Length is an output parameter.
explanations.b: Correct. Vehicle Arrival Rate indicates the number of arriving vehicles per minute.

questions.question: Q3. What does a green traffic signal indicate?

explanations.a: Vehicles stop during a red signal.
explanations.b: Correct. Green indicates permission to move through the intersection.
explanations.c: Green is primarily for vehicle movement.
explanations.d: Traffic signals do not indicate road closure.

questions.question: Q4. Traffic density is defined as:

explanations.a: Correct. Traffic density measures how many vehicles occupy available lanes.
explanations.b: This does not represent traffic density.
explanations.d: This is not the correct formula.

questions.question: Q5. Which factor directly affects the capacity of an intersection?

explanations.a: Correct. More lanes generally increase traffic handling capacity.
explanations.b: Vehicle color has no impact on capacity.
explanations.c: Driver age is not an input parameter in this experiment.
explanations.d: Road names do not affect traffic capacity.

questions.question: Q6. What is a queue in traffic engineering?

explanations.a: Correct. A queue is formed when vehicles wait for their turn to move.
explanations.b: Moving vehicles are not part of a queue.

questions.question: Q7. What is likely to happen when vehicle arrival rate increases significantly?

explanations.a: Correct. Higher arrival rates can create congestion and longer queues.

questions.question: Q8. Which of the following is an output parameter of the experiment?

explanations.a: Green Signal Time is an input.
explanations.b: Vehicle Arrival Rate is an input.
explanations.c: Correct. Queue Length is a calculated output.
explanations.d: Number of Lanes is an input.

questions.question: Q9. Why are traffic signals important in urban transportation systems?

answers.a: They help manage vehicle movement safely and efficiently

explanations.a: Correct. Traffic signals organize and regulate traffic flow.
explanations.b: Their purpose is to reduce congestion, not increase it.

questions.question: Q10. Why is traffic signal optimization considered an important component of a smart city?

answers.a: It improves transportation efficiency and reduces delays
answers.b: It eliminates all vehicles from roads
answers.c: It removes the need for intersections

explanations.a: Correct. Smart cities use optimized traffic systems to improve mobility and reduce congestion.
explanations.b: Vehicles remain an essential part of transportation.
